title: senecio friesii
intro: Senecio friesii, also known as Fries's ragwort, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to New Zealand. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a bitter tonic and purgative, although it contains toxic compounds called pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PAs) that can be hazardous in large doses. In moderation, the plant has been reported to have health benefits such as aiding digestion and reducing fever, as well as being used to treat various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Senecio friesii, when used under the guidance of a qualified practitioner, can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but it is crucial to exercise extreme caution due to its toxicity.
